text:
Queen Camilla has revealed she was brought to "tears" during an emotional reception at Clarence House amid King Charles’ cancer battle.

Queen Camilla revealed she was brought to "tears" after watching Great Britain’s triumphant equestrian team at the 2024 Paris Olympics during a poignant reception at Clarence House.

The Queen welcomed dozens of members of the British equestrian teams to her London home for the reception on Wednesday to celebrate the medallists from the Paris Olympics and Paralympics.

Britain won gold in both team eventing and team jumping at the Palace of Versailles, as well as three bronze medals in individual dressage, team dressage and individual eventing.

King Charles’s own niece Zara Tindall narrowly missed selection for Britain’s eventing teamafter earlier helping GB win a silver medal during the 2012 London Olympics.

Camilla, 77, serves as patron of the British Equestrian Foundation (BEF) and wowed during the reception in a navy blue dress, which she paired with black boots and her favourite Van Cleef & Arpels jewellery.

The Queen also addressed the athletes and admitted becoming emotional while watching the team compete.

"It made us all feel very proud to be British,” she said.

"I'm sorry I wasn't there but I was watching very keenly every bit of it on the television and it really did bring tears to the eyes.”

Camilla was previously a keen horsewoman but revealed in 2023 that she had given up riding at 75 “because I think I’m too old”.

The Queen’s intimate reception at Clarence House comes after insiders revealed that British Prime Minister Keir Starmer is keen to use the King and Queen to help charm new United States President Donald Trump.

Mr Trump haspreviously opened up about his personal affection for Britain’s Royal Family and praised the Queen as “fantastic”.

The US President even keeps a personal photo album of his 2017 state visit to the United Kingdom during his first term as president.

Mr Trump also wished the “beautiful” monarch well amid his ongoing cancer battle in November during a social media video filmed aboard the Trump campaign jet.

"Hopefully (Charles) is going to be well because he's a really good person,” he said.

title: 'It really did bring tears to the eyes': Queen Camilla's emotional speech to Paris Olympians after their equestrian victories | Sky News Australia
description: Queen Camilla revealed she was brought to "tears" after watching Great Britain’s triumphant equestrian team at the 2024 Paris Olympics during a poignant reception at Clarence House.
